data.title: Iranian President Asserts No Hostility Towards Gulf Nations
data.description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has stated that Iran harbors no hostility towards countries in the Gulf region. According to Jin10, Raisi emphasized the importance of regional cooperation and peaceful coexistence during a recent address. He highlighted Iran's commitment to fostering diplomatic relations and resolving conflicts through dialogue. Raisi's remarks come amid ongoing tensions in the region, where geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve. The Iranian leader reiterated his country's stance on promoting stability and security in the Gulf, urging neighboring nations to engage in constructive discussions. This statement aligns with Iran's broader foreign policy objectives of enhancing regional ties and reducing external influences. Observers note that Raisi's comments may signal a potential shift towards more collaborative regional interactions.

data.title: Binance Wallet now offers WTI crude oil (CL) perpetual contracts.
data.description: Binance Wallet announced on its X platform that it has launched WTI crude oil (CL) perpetual contracts. Users can trade crude oil derivatives on Binance Wallet Perpetuals. Currently, it supports 0% order fees, 1.2x Aster airdrop points, and the inclusion of trading volume in the $10,000 ASTER CL trading competition.

data.title: The UAE has announced the suspension of all flights to and from Dubai.
data.description: The UAE has announced the suspension of all flights to and from Dubai until further notice. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iranian President Apologizes to Neighboring Countries
data.description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has issued an apology to neighboring countries. According to Jin10, the apology comes amid ongoing regional tensions and diplomatic efforts to improve relations. The Iranian government has been actively seeking to mend ties with its neighbors, emphasizing the importance of regional cooperation and stability. This move is seen as part of a broader strategy to enhance diplomatic relations and foster peace in the region. The apology is expected to pave the way for further dialogue and collaboration between Iran and its neighboring states.

data.title: Heathrow CEO Faces Challenges Amid Disruptions at Key Hubs
data.description: Heathrow CEO Thomas Woldbye has successfully managed London’s primary airport through significant challenges, including a power outage and a cyberattack. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that Woldbye is now confronted with disruptions affecting major hubs such as Dubai. These challenges come as Heathrow continues to navigate complex operational landscapes, impacting international travel and logistics. The situation underscores the ongoing difficulties faced by global airports in maintaining seamless operations amid unforeseen disruptions.

data.title: ETC/BTC Pair Holds Steady at Long-Term Support Level
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. The ETC/BTC trading pair is currently positioned at a significant long-term support zone. Despite being in a downtrend, this level has consistently served as a robust support in the past. Traders are closely monitoring this area for potential market movements.

data.title: Circle CEO: We have already achieved $68 million in internal settlements using USDC, and the real-time model will be rolled out to more businesses.
data.description: Jeremy Allaire, CEO of stablecoin issuer Circle, stated on the X platform that Circle has begun using its platform to process internal fund settlements. Through USDC and Circle Mint, approximately $68 million in cross-company settlements were completed between eight entities in less than 30 minutes and can operate 24/7. This method replaces the traditional fiat currency wire transfer process, which typically takes 1-3 days, and offers complete control and auditing capabilities. Currently, 90% of the company's transfer pricing settlements can be completed within one day. As Circle Mint continues to update its functionality, this real-time settlement model based on stablecoins is expected to be available to more enterprises in the future.

data.title: Sky Co-Founder Opens $5.89 Million in Crude Oil Long Futures on Hyperliquid
data.description: Sky co-founder Rune Christensen has initiated approximately $5.89 million in crude oil long futures positions on the decentralized derivatives platform Hyperliquid. According to NS3.AI, about an hour ago, a wallet linked to Christensen transferred around 4.01 million USDC to the platform before executing the trades. The positions consist of roughly $5.71 million in WTI crude longs and about $180,000 in Brent crude longs, with entry prices near $92.08 and $92.16, respectively.

data.title: Iran Conducts Precision Strikes on Kurdish Separatist Bases in Iraq
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched precision strikes on three separatist bases located in Iraq's Kurdistan region. According to Jin10, the strikes occurred at 4:30 AM local time on March 7. An Iranian armed forces spokesperson warned that any actions by separatist groups threatening Iran's territorial integrity would be met with devastating retaliation.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin may drop another 30% this year, and has now entered a deep bear market phase.
data.description: CK Zheng, founder of crypto hedge fund ZX Squared Capital, stated that Bitcoin has entered a deep bear market. With the increasing influence of the "four-year cycle," the price could fall by approximately 30% in 2026. Previously, Bitcoin reached an all-time high of over $126,000 in October 2025, approximately 16-18 months after its April 2024 halving. It has since fallen back to around $68,000, largely conforming to the four-year cycle pattern centered around the halving. The psychology of retail investors chasing rallies during market frenzies and selling during panics has reinforced this bear market cycle. Meanwhile, institutional adoption remains limited, with crypto ETFs and digital asset reserve companies accounting for only about 10% of the overall market. In a bear market environment, some companies holding Bitcoin as an asset reserve may be forced to sell assets due to debt repayment pressures, further exacerbating downward market pressure. (CoinDesk)

data.title: International oil prices surged 35.6% in a single week, breaking through $90, marking the largest weekly increase in history.
data.description: US WTI crude oil futures rose 12.67% to $91.27 per barrel, a 35.6% increase from before the escalation of the Iraq War on February 28, marking the largest weekly gain for this futures contract since its inception in 1983. UK Brent crude oil futures rose 9.26% to $93.32 per barrel, a 27.5% increase for the week, the largest weekly gain since 1990. Historically, after the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine conflict in late February 2022, both Brent and WTI saw weekly gains of approximately 25% in early March; during the Gulf War in August 1990, Brent spot prices rose as much as 26% in a single week. The Iraq War has now broken all previous records for weekly crude oil gains. (Aastocks)

data.title: U.S. and Israeli Airstrikes in Isfahan Result in Casualties
data.description: According to Jin10, reports from Iran's Tasnim News Agency indicate that airstrikes conducted by the United States and Israel in Isfahan have resulted in the deaths of eight individuals. The incident underscores ongoing tensions in the region, highlighting the complex geopolitical dynamics at play. Further details about the airstrikes and their implications remain limited at this time.

data.title: Tornado Strikes Michigan, Causing Fatalities and Damage
data.description: A tornado hit Michigan on March 6, resulting in at least four fatalities and injuring over a dozen people, according to U.S. media reports. According to Jin10, the severe weather event caused significant damage, including downed trees and damaged homes in the southwestern part of the state. In response, Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer has activated the state's emergency operations center to coordinate efforts to address the aftermath of the tornado.

data.title: Hong Kong Experiences Third Multi-Million Dollar Cash Robbery in Three Months
data.description: Two individuals were robbed at knifepoint inside Sheung Wan MTR Station in Hong Kong after exchanging approximately HKD 10 million at a currency exchange. According to NS3.AI, this incident marks the third similar multi-million dollar cash robbery at a currency exchange in Hong Kong within the last three months. Authorities are investigating the pattern of these robberies to enhance security measures and prevent future occurrences.

data.title: XRP Currently Offers a Strong Risk:Reward Setup as Downside Fuel Nearly Gone
data.description:
The current XRP position could offer one of the strongest risk:reward setups as downside fuel continues to deplete. XRP has witnessed one of its longest stretches of downward price action, having recorded five consecutive monthly red candlesticks, a bearish development that last played out in late 2016 to early 2017. Visit Website
